content.seo_title: For Students: Passive Income to Solve Coding Debugging Blocks
content.seo_description: Learn how to generate passive income while overcoming debugging hurdles. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for student developers.
content.answer_block: Stop letting debugging blocks kill your momentum. By leveraging AI agents like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can automate error resolution, allowing you to focus on shipping micro-SaaS products. This pipeline shifts your mindset from 'stuck student' to 'efficient builder,' turning your code into scalable passive income streams.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and your codebase is a graveyard of unhandled exceptions. You are trapped in a loop of 'undefined is not a function,' watching your motivation evaporate alongside your project timeline. It is the classic student's crucible: the gap between brilliant ideas and the technical grit required to actually ship them.

But here is the secret the pros won't tell you: you don't need to be a debugger; you need to be an architect. By integrating autonomous AI agents into your stack, you bypass the hours of frustration that break most developers. We are going to transform your debugging process into a production engine, paving the way for your first passive income stream.

steps.title: Define Your Micro-SaaS Scope
steps.description: Narrow your project to a single, high-value function to minimize complexity and maximize revenue potential.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this project idea [Insert Idea] and break it down into a Minimum Viable Product that can be built in under 48 hours.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --init --project-scope 'Build MVP for [Idea] focusing on single-function utility'

steps.title: Automate Error Detection
steps.description: Configure your environment to catch errors before they stall your development cycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a robust error-handling architecture that logs stack traces and provides plain-English suggestions for fixes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Implement global error boundary and logging system'

steps.title: Debug with AI-Driven Insight
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a root-cause analysis on your existing errors rather than manually guessing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current error logs and suggest a refactor that prevents this specific bug from recurring.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --debug 'Analyze current errors and refactor for stability'

steps.title: Build the Passive Income Layer
steps.description: Integrate a payment gateway like Stripe to turn your functional code into a revenue-generating asset.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure checkout flow using Stripe API that triggers upon successful feature completion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Integrate Stripe checkout flow into the current project structure'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Push your code to a live environment so your users can access your tool while you sleep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Prepare a deployment script for Vercel/Railway that automates environment variable injection and build processes.

pro_tips: Treat your error logs as data; if an error occurs twice, it is a design flaw, not a bug.
Use Claude Code to write tests for your bugs before you fix them; this guarantees the issue never returns.
Focus on 'boring' problems—B2B utility tools generate passive income faster than consumer apps.
Document your debugging process as a blog post; it builds your portfolio and authority simultaneously.
Never manually fix a bug if an AI can explain why it happened; deep learning is the real value of the exercise.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, monitor the logs for 24 hours, gather user feedback, and iterate on one feature to increase your conversion rate.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder with a live, revenue-generating product. The path from 'debugging frustration' to 'passive income' is now open. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
